question.text: Fatima, a Muslim woman, dies leaving behind her husband Irfan, two daughters Zara and Ayesha, and a son Kabir. Under Muslim personal law, what share does Irfan (husband) receive?

answer.text: 1/2 of the estate since he is the spouse
1/4 of the estate since there are children
1/3 of the estate as per Wasiyat rules
1/8 of the estate as the surviving spouse

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Muslim law, among the 12 sharers, a husband gets 1/2 if there are NO children, and 1/4 if there ARE children. Since Fatima has children (Zara, Ayesha, and Kabir), Irfan gets 1/4. **The Trap:** The 1/2 share applies only when there are no children. The 1/8 share is for a WIFE (not husband) when there are children. Candidates who reverse husband/wife shares will pick the wrong answer. **Cross-Exam Context:** Muslim succession shares are specifically tested in NISM X-B. Remember: Husband (1/2 or 1/4) vs Wife (1/4 or 1/8) — husband always gets double what wife gets. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Muslim succession math is precise. A son gets double what a daughter gets. Always use a chart when advising Muslim families on inheritance shares.
